pages.full_text:
__April 6th__ Charles telephoned from next door van to say hello. His van's warm. Simon said we can look at new, insulated vans in autumn.
	Charles left his electronic boxes outside van + I tripped, barked shin badly. Blood absorbed into cord jeans. We drove to Newark Park. Still bloody freezing. I'm wearing thermal vest, long sleeved T, cardigan, aron cardigan, quilted waistcoat + coat, hat + gloves…. still cold. After coffee we sat in [strikethrough: so] sun + I need hat as sunshade.
	Amazing views from house. Tudor hunting lodge, extra building. Loos – long drop outside building + clever use of gutter rainwater to flush but waste. Hooks for clothes because ammonia from piss wd. get rid of bugs. Burning gorse in bread ovens raises temperature. Lovely plaster work. Texan Robert Power restored house 1970.
	Lunch back at van.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. 'April 6th' is underlined. 'aron' likely means 'Arran' (brand of knitwear). Robert Power is mentioned as restoring the house in 1970. April 7th is noted as Sunday on the right page — in volume B (2003–2019), April 7th falls on a Sunday in 2013 and 2019; cannot confirm year from page content alone.

pages.full_text:
After a lunch time nap (Charles + Eve) + [?sunbathe] (Simon) Charles worked + Simon + Eve walked round a steep hillside to an area – Breakheart Quarry – owned by the Nuclear Decommissioning Area – covered in silver birch trees + primroses.
	We are off to a pub 400m away this evening, but [strikethrough: no] way, way down in the valley. The New Inn.
	It was only 400 m away, but one of the steepest tracks – door to door – that we've ever been on. The wood is called Kill End Wood. Was the mill wind at the top of the hill? Meal OK ish. Staff nice.
__April 7th Sunday.__
	Less cold. Charles left to measure hop [?with] for his computer on a farm near William.
	. We'd like to come here with family. It's less than 1hr 20 min. for everyone (except Ater – 2½ hours) Nearly in Heartbreak Quarry room to ride bikes. Campsite is a huge field
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-right corner. 'April 7th Sunday' is underlined. April 7th falls on a Sunday in 2013 and 2019 within volume B range (2003–2019). 'Nuclear Decommissioning Area' may be 'Nuclear Decommissioning Authority'. 'hop with' is partially legible — could be 'hopwith' or 'hop wilt' — context unclear. 'Ater' is likely a person's name or nickname. 'Breakheart Quarry' and 'Heartbreak Quarry' appear to refer to the same location, spelled differently within the same entry. Text continues beyond bottom of page — entry is not complete on this spread. 'near William' is unclear — may be a place name or person's name.
